headline: HEAVY SLOP WARNING: PHANTOM-BYLINE FRONT SETTLES OVER THE NEWSWIRE FENS AS ARTICLES SIGNED BY REPORTERS WHO DO NOT EXIST FILE DISPATCHES FROM EVENTS THAT DID NOT OCCUR
advisory: A persistent authorship inversion has developed across the news basin, in which finished articles precede the existence of anyone to have written them. Residents are advised that a warm, professional-sounding byline is no longer evidence of a warm, professional-sounding human. The Service reminds the public that a headshot rendered at 512 pixels and a bio reading 'covers technology, culture, and the human stories in between' should be treated as an unverified pressure reading. Verify sourcing before sharing; corrections issued after the fact tend to evaporate before reaching the residents they were meant to reach.

regional_outlook.conditions: Dense phantom-byline fog through the morning, with fabricated quotes drifting in low over the wetlands. Visibility on primary sourcing near zero until an actual editor is located.
Standing pools of near-identical 'breaking' coverage, all citing one another. Expect circular attribution and a persistent smell of aggregation.
Warm updrafts of self-congratulation as synthetic correspondents announce they are 'thrilled to share' articles no one wrote. Broetry advisory remains in effect.
Gusty screenshot activity carrying the phantom dispatches far beyond their source. Debunks trail the originals by twelve to eighteen hours.
Newsletter runoff heavy with 'curated' links to stories that self-corrected overnight. Scattered unsubscribe fronts developing by evening.

specimen.status: Widespread and multiplying; now nesting in outlets that once employed people.
specimen.habitat: Content farms, hollowed-out local news domains, and any masthead where the 'Our Team' page loads slightly too fast.
specimen.field_marks: A friendly non-specific headshot; a three-clause bio promising to cover 'tech, culture, and everything in between'; an email address that bounces; and a portfolio of forty articles published in a single afternoon.
specimen.call: With over a decade of experience, I'm passionate about bringing you the stories that matter — reach out anytime, I'd love to connect.
specimen.confusion_species: Easily mistaken for an actual overworked freelance journalist. Tell them apart by asking a follow-up question: the freelancer replies, exhausted, at 11 p.m.; the phantom does not reply, because there is no there there.

spotter_tip: Click the byline. A real reporter has a messy trail — old bylines, a strange podcast appearance, a typo they never fixed. A phantom has forty flawless articles and no past before last Tuesday.
tomorrow: Phantom-byline fog thinning by midday, replaced by a fast-moving correction front that few residents will ever see.

confirmed_sighting.report: An industry tracker continues to catalogue AI-related errors, fabrications, and scandals in journalism, documenting a running series of incidents in which AI-generated content has caused problems for publishers. The Service notes the log is described as regularly updated, which is itself a reading.
